title: centaurea scabiosa subsp. cephalariifolia
intro: Centaurea scabiosa subsp. cephalariifolia, also known as the Cephalaria scabiosa, is a subspecies of the scabious plant, which is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of the Cephalaria scabiosa can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
